name: El Atance Reservoir Loop Trail
name_original: Vuelta al embalse de El Atance
description: This difficult 14.3-mile hike around the El Atance reservoir offers views of the submerged village and the Río Salado Canyon.
region_intro: Hiking around Riofrío Del Llano offers diverse natural landscapes, characterized by the Sierra Norte Natural Park with its centenary trees and panoramic views. The region also features less-traveled paths along local rivers and more challenging terrain within the Las Cumbres Natural Reserve, which includes imposing mountains and deep valleys. These varied environments provide a range of hiking experiences for different skill levels. Best hiking trails around Riofrío Del Llano The most popular hiking route is Atienza Castle and Camino del Cid Loop, an easy 3.5 miles (5.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 37 minutes to complete. This route offers views of historical landmarks and the surrounding countryside. Another top favourite among local hikers is El Atance Reservoir Loop Trail, a difficult 14.3 miles (23.0 km) path. This trail explores the area around the El Atance Reservoir, providing a longer and more demanding experience. Local hikers also love the Highlights of Atienza Village loop from Atienza, a 2.6 miles (4.2 km) trail leading through the historic village of Atienza, often completed in about 1 hour 11 minutes. Hiking around Riofrío Del Llano is defined by diverse landscapes including the Sierra Norte Natural Park, local rivers, and the mountainous terrain of Las Cumbres Natural Reserve. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy village loops to more challenging reservoir trails. The routes in Riofrío Del Llano are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. More than 10 hikers have used komoot to explore Riofrío Del Llano's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of landscapes will I encounter while hiking around Riofrío Del Llano?
A: The region offers a diverse range of landscapes. You can expect to hike through the majestic centenary trees and enjoy panoramic views within the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra Norte Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;. There are also less-traveled paths along local rivers and more challenging terrain with imposing mountains and deep valleys in the &lt;strong&gt;Las Cumbres Natural Reserve&lt;/strong&gt;.

Q: Are there hiking options for different skill levels in Riofrío Del Llano?
A: Yes, Riofrío Del Llano caters to all levels of hikers. The area features trails ranging from easy paths suitable for beginners and families to demanding ascents for experienced outdoor enthusiasts. For example, you can find easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025864490/atienza-castle-and-camino-del-cid-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atienza Castle and Camino del Cid Loop&lt;/a&gt;, as well as more difficult ones such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025875954/el-atance-reservoir-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Atance Reservoir Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features can I see while hiking in Riofrío Del Llano?
A: Hikers can explore significant natural features like the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra Norte Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt; and the mountainous landscapes of the &lt;strong&gt;Las Cumbres Natural Reserve&lt;/strong&gt;. The area also features several reservoirs, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5388806" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañamares River Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5440295" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Atance Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, offering picturesque waterside views.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the trails?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. You can discover historical sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2054767" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Atienza&lt;/a&gt; and explore the charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4442873" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Highlights of Atienza Village&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;strong&gt;El Molino Viejo Trail&lt;/strong&gt; also features restored old mills, adding a unique historical dimension to your hike.

Q: Are there any circular walks available in Riofrío Del Llano?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025864490/atienza-castle-and-camino-del-cid-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atienza Castle and Camino del Cid Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 5.7 km trail. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38153898" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Highlights of Atienza Village loop from Atienza&lt;/a&gt;, a shorter 4.2 km walk through the historic village.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Riofrío Del Llano?
A: The diverse landscapes of Riofrío Del Llano make it suitable for hiking across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, while summer can be ideal for higher-altitude trails. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Riofrío Del Llano suitable for families with children?
A: Yes, the area offers simple paths suitable for children and families. Many trails provide an enjoyable outdoor experience without being overly strenuous, allowing everyone to appreciate the natural beauty. The &lt;strong&gt;Sierra Norte Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, for instance, has well-marked routes suitable for various ages.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails around Riofrío Del Llano?
A: Many natural areas in Spain, including those around Riofrío Del Llano, are generally dog-friendly, especially on marked trails. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in natural parks or near wildlife, and to check specific regulations for any protected areas you plan to visit.

Q: What is the typical duration of hikes in Riofrío Del Llano?
A: Hikes in Riofrío Del Llano vary greatly in duration. You can find shorter walks, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38153898" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Highlights of Atienza Village loop from Atienza&lt;/a&gt;, which takes about 1 hour 11 minutes. Longer and more demanding trails, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025875954/el-atance-reservoir-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Atance Reservoir Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, can take several hours to complete.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Riofrío Del Llano?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Hikers often praise the diverse natural landscapes, the variety of trails for different skill levels, and the breathtaking scenery found within the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra Norte Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Las Cumbres Natural Reserve&lt;/strong&gt;.

Q: Are there any moderate difficulty hikes available?
A: Yes, for those looking for a balanced challenge, there are moderate options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38157574" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Carabias loop from Iglesia Románica del Salvador&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 17.8 km trail that offers a good distance and elevation gain without being overly strenuous.

Q: Are there opportunities for longer, more demanding hikes?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the region offers routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025875954/el-atance-reservoir-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Atance Reservoir Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 23.0 km path. The area also intersects with longer routes such as the 'Ruta de la Lana Stage 17: Baides-Atienza', providing considerable distances for those looking for multi-hour excursions.
tips: Views of the canyon formed by the erosion of the Salado River with the Peñas de los Abantos and the Alto del Cuerno on the sides.
Reservoir located in the bed of the Salado River, in the province of Guadalajara, inaugurated in 1997 to irrigate the land. The town of El Atance was sunk under its waters.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Tour around the El Atance reservoir?
faqs.answer: The Tour around the El Atance reservoir is a substantial hike, generally taking around 6 hours and 10 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this hike?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as difficult. It demands very good physical condition, steady footing, appropriate footwear, and some experience in mountain hiking. While there are easier paths in the broader El Atance area, this specific tour is challenging.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail around El Atance reservoir?
faqs.answer: The trail is located in the vicinity of Sigüenza, La Sierra, Guadalajara. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, you would typically look for parking near the starting point of the route, often in a nearby village or designated trailhead parking if available. Public transport options are generally limited in such rural areas.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary scenic feature is the El Atance Reservoir itself. You will also encounter the View of the Río Salado Canyon. A unique aspect is the historical significance of the submerged village of El Atance, which lies beneath the reservoir's waters.

faqs.question: Is the Tour around the El Atance reservoir dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, trails in this region are dog-friendly, but it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas where wildlife might be present or near livestock. Ensure your dog is fit enough for a difficult and long hike.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no mention of permits or entrance fees required to hike around the El Atance reservoir. Access to the natural area is typically free.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike around El Atance reservoir would generally be during spring or autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, and winters might bring colder weather, making the difficult terrain more challenging.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this hike?
faqs.answer: The terrain around El Atance reservoir offers a diverse hiking experience. While specific details about the varied terrain are not extensively provided, the existence of different difficulty levels for trails in the area suggests a mix of paths, from more accessible sections to challenging mountain-like conditions, requiring steady footing.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its classification as 'difficult' and a duration of over 6 hours, this specific tour is generally not recommended for families with young children or those seeking an easy outing. There might be shorter, easier paths closer to the reservoir, but this full tour requires good physical condition.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, approximately 26% of this route overlaps with sections of the official trail known as "Camino de Santiago de la Lana.Senderista(Var.Sigüenza)".

faqs.question: Are there any specific tips for navigating this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the length and difficulty, it's highly recommended to plan your itinerary in advance and utilize navigation with offline maps. This ensures you stay on track and can manage unexpected situations, especially in areas with potentially limited mobile signal.

waypoints.name: View of the Río Salado Canyon
